taxRateNote: Municipal portion only (the total tax bill also includes school, regional district, library, and BC Assessment levies). BC publishes rates as mills (per $1,000) rather than per $100.
sourceLabel: City of Nanaimo 2025-2029 Financial Plan (adopted May 2025)
dataStatusNote: Verified from the City of Nanaimo's 2025-2029 Financial Plan: operating budget $214M (a $13M increase over 2024), 168 RCMP members under contract (with 4 new members added in 2025), 20 new firefighters January 1, 2025 supporting the Fire Master Plan, plus 6 new Community Safety Officers. Nanaimo contracts the RCMP for policing — same model as Moncton (Codiac RCMP), so the public-safety bucket is directly comparable. Water and sewer run as a separate user-fee utility (not in general operating). Per-bucket amounts are proportional estimates pending line-item transcription from the official Financial Plan PDF.

nativeDepartments.name: RCMP contract — 168 members (estimated ~$36M based on contract size)
Nanaimo Fire Rescue (estimated ~$30M, expanded by 20 firefighters in 2025)
Bylaw, Community Safety Officers, emergency preparedness (estimated)
Funded as a separate user-fee utility (water + sewer + sanitation revenues, paid on the utility bill, not property tax)
Corporate Services — HR, IT, Legal, Communications, Clerk, Council (estimated)
Fiscal Services — debt servicing, capital from operating, reserve contributions (estimated)
Grants to outside organizations, contingency, other (estimated)
